description_long: This object is a brass-colored trumpet with a smoothly curved, coiled body and a large, flared bell at one end. The bell is wide and gently transitions from the tubing, giving the instrument a classic, bold appearance. Three piston-style valves are positioned centrally along the top of the instrument, each with a visible button for actuation. The leadpipe curves gracefully from the mouthpiece area, looping around the body before connecting to the main tuning slide, which is U-shaped and located beneath the valves. The surface has a metallic sheen, suggesting a polished brass or similar alloy construction. The overall design is streamlined and functional, typical of a marching or concert band trumpet.
description: A brass-colored trumpet with a wide flared bell, three central valves, and a smoothly coiled body. The surface is metallic and polished, with a classic, streamlined design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full coiled body, three valves, and the wide flared bell.
description_view_1: Front view looking directly into the bell, with the circular opening dominating the image.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the bell flare and the coiled tubing, with valves visible from the side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the mouthpiece area and the curvature of the tubing, with the bell mostly hidden.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide with a gradual, smooth flare, creating a prominent opening.
attribute_valve_configuration: Three piston valves are arranged in a straight line along the top center of the instrument.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is gently curved, looping smoothly from the mouthpiece to the main body.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: The main tuning slide is U-shaped and located beneath the valves, connecting the leadpipe to the main tubing.
